title: Taken for a ride
visual_consistency: A warm educational old-bus travel comedy illustration in the same gentle watercolor children's book style used by the course: hand-painted texture, soft brush strokes, slight grain, warm pastel color, painterly lighting, and cinematic storybook composition. Avoid flat vector art, icon-like drawing, simple line art, hard-edged classroom clip art, or schematic diagrams. No readable text, letters, numbers, route signs, destination boards, bus-stop names, ticket text, map labels, speech bubbles, captions, UI panels, empty boxes, blank label spaces, or fake writing anywhere in the image. The recurring narrator is a polite young adult passenger in his late 20s, light skin, short chestnut-brown hair, long oval face, slim average build, wearing a moss-green jacket over a cream shirt and dark trousers, carrying a small brown travel bag; he looks curious, relaxed, and gently absent-minded because he enjoys the view. The recurring old bus conductor is a middle-aged man, light skin, friendly round face, short graying hair, average build, wearing a navy conductor-style jacket and cap with a leather ticket bag and small metal ticket puncher; he is helpful but forgetful, never malicious. The bus is an old British countryside bus interior with warm wooden trim, broad windows, simple seats, soft daylight, and rural scenery outside. The driver appears only as a secondary figure at the wheel when needed. Mood is light, safe, and mildly comic, with no traffic danger, no frightening lost-person tone, and no readable text.

frames.prompt: Inside the same old British countryside bus, the recurring young adult passenger in a moss-green jacket and cream shirt steps aboard with his small brown travel bag. The driver is visible as a secondary figure seated at the front wheel, while the recurring middle-aged conductor in a navy jacket and cap stands in the aisle with a leather ticket bag and small metal puncher, listening politely. A few ordinary passengers sit nearby. Make the old driver-and-conductor bus system visually clear through body positions and props, not through text. Warm watercolor storybook style, gentle daylight, safe light comedy. No readable text, no letters, no numbers, no route signs, no destination boards, no bus-stop names, no ticket text, no map labels, no speech bubbles, no captions, no UI panels, no empty boxes, and no blank label spaces.

The same young passenger sits near the front of the same old bus, absorbed in the countryside view through the broad windows, still wearing his moss-green jacket and holding the small brown travel bag nearby. Green fields, hedges, and soft rural daylight pass outside. The same conductor in navy uniform is farther down the aisle, busy helping another passenger or checking a ticket, slightly distracted but kind. The scene should explain why the passenger relaxes and misses his stop, without making him look foolish. Warm watercolor storybook style. No readable text, no letters, no numbers, no route signs, no destination boards, no bus-stop names, no ticket text, no map labels, no speech bubbles, no captions, no UI panels, no empty boxes, and no blank label spaces.

At the quiet route end, inside the same old bus, the same young passenger turns around from his front seat with a surprised but safe comic expression and realizes the bus is empty. Empty seats stretch behind him. The same conductor in navy uniform stands nearby with the leather ticket bag and puncher, beginning to realize he forgot something. The outside through the windows suggests a calm rural terminal or turning area without any readable signs or labels. Keep the mood bright, mild, and not frightening. Warm watercolor storybook style. No readable text, no letters, no numbers, no route signs, no destination boards, no bus-stop names, no ticket text, no map labels, no speech bubbles, no captions, no UI panels, no empty boxes, and no blank label spaces.

Inside the same old bus just before it heads back, the same conductor in navy uniform gently gestures toward the front as if explaining that the bus is returning, while the same young passenger stays seated with his small brown travel bag and gives a resigned, lightly amused smile. The driver remains secondary at the wheel. The warm bus interior and rural daylight remain consistent, and the ending feels practical and funny rather than angry. Warm watercolor storybook style. No readable text, no letters, no numbers, no route signs, no destination boards, no bus-stop names, no ticket text, no map labels, no speech bubbles, no captions, no UI panels, no empty boxes, and no blank label spaces.
